# ANALYSIS: V14E chop-zone 5/04 P&L impact (OTM-2 puts)

**Filed:** 2026-05-24  
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)  
**Type:** analysis  
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-RATIFICATION per Rule 9)

## Hypothesis

The chop gate (blocking 10:xx-11:xx entries for v14e bearish setups) would prevent the medium-confidence 10:05 entry on 5/04 and allow the high-confidence 11:15 entry. We hypothesize that the 11:15 entry yields a higher P&L than the 10:05 entry due to better alignment with the bearish trend, resulting in a net positive impact on the J anchor day 5/04.

## Mechanism

Without chop gate: engine takes the first v14e bearish signal at 10:05 (medium confidence) and enters an OTM-2 put trade with production stop -0.08. The first-entry-lock then blocks any subsequent signals (including the 11:15 high-confidence signal) for the day.

With chop gate: the 10:05 signal is blocked due to elevated threshold during chop hours (10:xx-11:xx). The engine then takes the 11:15 high-confidence signal and enters an OTM-2 put trade with the same stop.

## Expected impact on OP-16 anchors

| J day | Current engine behavior (no chop gate) | Proposed behavior (with chop gate) | Delta |
|---|---|---|---|
| 5/04 winner | unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest | unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest | unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest |

(Note: 4/29, 5/01, 5/05, 5/06, 5/07 are unaffected by the chop gate as they either have no v14e bearish signals in the chop zone or the signals occur outside 10:xx-11:xx.)

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** qty=28 requires $25K+; $1K paper ~= 14% headline
2. **Sample bias:** Single-day analysis (2026-05-04) using OOS spy bars; high overfit risk due to cherry-picking a known J anchor day
3. **Out-of-sample:** NEEDS-OOS (analysis uses the same day's bars for entry/exit simulation; no walk-forward or held-out window applied)
4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (simulate_trade_real not validated against real OPRA fills for OTM-2 puts on 2026-05-04)
5. **Failure modes:** 
   - Worst case: 11:15 entry results in large loss (e.g., -$500) turning a winning day into a losing day
   - Blow-up scenario: chop gate exacerbates losses if 11:15 entry fails while 10:05 entry would have been profitable
6. **Concentration:** 5/04 alone represents 47.3% of max possible edge_capture ($730/$1542); changes to this day disproportionately impact overall edge_capture

## Pre-merge gate

- Stage-1 backtest on OOS spy bars for 2026-05-04 with OTM-2 puts and production stop -0.08 for both entry times
- Validate simulate_trade_real against real OPRA fills for top 3 J days (4/29, 5/01, 5/04) ensuring P&L estimates within ±20% of real-fills
- Confirm chop gate does not affect other J days (4/29, 5/01, 5/05, 5/06, 5/07) via bar-time analysis

## Confidence

2 / 10 -- No actual simulation results for OTM-2 puts on 5/04; analysis is entirely hypothetical until Stage-1 backtest is performed

## Pre-existing leaderboard impact

This analysis informs candidate #17 (V14E_BEAR_TIME_OF_DAY_GATE) which is already deployed as a watcher-only change per OP-22. Quantifying the 5/04 impact is relevant for J's consideration of ratifying the chop gate in heartbeat.md (per candidate #17's pre-merge gate). No conflict with existing candidates; purely additive analysis for decision-making.
